I am a final-year Mathematics PhD candidate at the University of Washington, advised by Sándor Kovács and Farbod Shokrieh. My research develops tools at the intersection of algebraic geometry, topological data analysis, and machine learning — with applications to neural network interpretability and quantitative modeling.

I am also a Research Scientist at the Georgia Tech Research Institute, where I build algorithms for measuring geometric and topological structure in high-dimensional spaces. My work there led to a published tool for analyzing ReLU neural network activation patterns, accepted to the Proceedings of Machine Learning Research (2025).
I am currently seeking quantitative research, ML engineering, and research scientist roles starting June 2026.
